rawContent: Task: 419-260405-054539 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 419-260405-054539 (rerun from April 5) completed a JWT auth redesign with RBAC migration, with all 7 pipeline stages completing in 4s and 0 retries each. The build, verify, review, and review-fix stages all succeeded without retries. However, the verify stage reported 1 test file failed (117 passed, 1 failed with 1 error) yet the pipeline proceeded through review-fix to ship without blocking. This is the seventh consecutive run exhibiting the identical pattern where verify-stage failures do not halt pipeline progression to ship. | Pattern: This is the seventh consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, and now 419-rerun) exhibiting the identical flaw: verify stage reports FAIL due to test failures, yet the pipeline proceeds through review-fix and ship without blocking. Five prior retrospectives across two days recommended implementing a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status, but the flaw persists unchanged. The autofix stage cannot resolve this class of test failures, and the pipeline lacks a mechanism to halt when verify reports FAIL. | Suggestion: Implement the conditional gate in the ship stage that was recommended in five prior retrospectives without implementation: add a verifyExitCode check in the ship stage template that reads the verify stage's exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing automatic shipment of code that failed test validation. | Flaw: verify — Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
